title: H_001 LPP source-power ceiling — representative LPP budget (~250 W class → ~1.5 kW pushed) falls ~2 kW short of the ~3.5 kW the 6.5 nm optics demand (H_002 ×14) requires. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — baseline 250 W, ceiling 1500 W, demand 3548 W (gap 2048 W). LPP power-scaling alone insufficient for 6.5 nm on this representative budget; engineering-horizon wall (reopenable). 5/5 PASS.

title: H_002 BEUV (6.5 nm) mirror-throughput wall — same 11-mirror column passes ~14x fewer photons at 6.5 nm (La/B R=0.55) than 13.5 nm (Mo/Si R=0.70), forcing ~14x more source power. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — power_multiplier=14.19x (T_euv=0.0198, T_beuv=0.00139; N=7→5.41x), 5/5 falsifiers PASS. Optics-side source wall before emitter/resist penalties.

title: H_003 NA vs depth-of-focus trade — NA 0.33→0.55 prints 1.67x finer but loses 2.78x focus depth; DoF penalty (NA²) strictly steeper than resolution gain (NA¹). SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — res 12.27→7.36 nm (1.667x), DoF 61.98→22.31 nm (2.778x = (NA_hi/NA_lo)²). Geometric root of the ultra-thin-resist wall. 5/5 PASS.

title: H_004 compact plasma-wakefield accelerator as light-source enabler — Dawson E_0=96.16 GV/m (~3200x RF) reaches 1 GeV in ~1 cm vs ~33 m. Reference-matched to demiurge cern-accelerator. SUPPORTED 5/5.
verdict: 🟢 SUPPORTED — E_0=96.15920 GV/m (|Δ|=0 vs demiurge ref), gradient 3205x RF, length 3205x shorter. Compactness enabler for a tabletop EUV source; photon-generation downstream (L2). 5/5 PASS.
